docs.title: ogdf-py
docs.text:
Python bindings for the Open Graph Drawing Framework (OGDF), built with nanobind.

This is a curated subset of OGDF, not a full wrapper: enough to build graphs, run layout and core graph algorithms, style and draw them, and read/write common graph file formats. See Coverage for exactly what is and is not included.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install ogdf-py

Binary wheels are published for Linux (x86_64, aarch64), macOS (x86_64, arm64), and Windows (AMD64) on CPython 3.10-3.14.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
import ogdf

# Build a graph and run a force-directed layout.
g = ogdf.Graph()
ogdf.random_planar_connected_graph(g, 30, 45)
ga = ogdf.GraphAttributes(g)
ogdf.FMMMLayout().call(ga)

# Export to SVG.
ogdf.draw_svg(ga, "graph.svg")

docs.title: What's included
docs.text:
• Graph model - Graph, Node, Edge, node/edge iteration, and NodeArray/EdgeArray attribute arrays.

  • Attributes & styling - coordinates, size, labels, colors, shapes, fill patterns, stroke, edge arrows, and bends.

  • Layouts - layered, force-directed, stress/MDS, planarization, orthogonal, planar straight-line, tree, and circular.

  • Algorithms - connectivity and structure tests, components, shortest paths, spanning tree, flow, cut, matching, coloring, and planarity.

  • Generators - complete, bipartite, wheel, cube, grid, Petersen, trees, and several random families.

  • File I/O - GML, GraphML, DOT, GEXF, GDF, TLP interchange, plus SVG and TikZ drawing output.

See Getting Started for a fuller tour, the Gallery for example drawings, and Coverage for exactly what is included.

docs.title: Coverage
docs.text:
This page tracks what OGDF functionality is exposed by ogdf-py. It is a living checklist: ogdf-py is a curated subset, so most of OGDF is intentionally excluded.

Legend: [x] = bound and available in Python, [ ] = not (yet) bound.

Scope rationale. The binding targets OGDF's genuine differentiators for Python users - graph drawing and planarity - plus a core set of common graph algorithms (even where these overlap networkx/scipy) for a self-contained experience. Exotic or specialized algorithms, and large subsystems (clustering, UML, hypergraphs, upward planarity), are excluded unless there is concrete demand. Contributions that move an item from [ ] to [x] are welcome.

docs.title: Graph model and attributes
docs.text:
• Graph, Node, Edge, node/edge iteration

  • NodeArray / EdgeArray (int, double, bool)

  • GraphAttributes: coordinates, width/height, labels

  • Styling: Color, Shape, StrokeType, FillPattern, EdgeArrow

  • Node fill/stroke color, shape, fill pattern, stroke width

  • Edge stroke, arrow type, bends

  • 3D coordinates (z)

  • ClusterGraph / ClusterGraphAttributes

  • GraphCopy / GraphReduction

  • Combinatorial embedding / dual graph

  • Hypergraphs

docs.title: Layout algorithms
docs.text:
• SugiyamaLayout (layered / hierarchical)

  • FMMMLayout (fast multipole multilevel, force-directed)

  • GEMLayout (force-directed)

  • SpringEmbedderKK (Kamada-Kawai)

  • StressMinimization

  • PivotMDS

  • PlanarizationLayout (with optional orthogonal routing via OrthoLayout)

  • SchnyderLayout (planar straight-line grid)

  • TreeLayout

  • CircularLayout

  • BalloonLayout

  • RadialTreeLayout

  • BertaultLayout

  • DavidsonHarelLayout

  • MultilevelLayout / ModularMultilevelMixer

  • FastMultipoleEmbedder

  • NodeRespecterLayout

  • PlanarStraightLayout / PlanarDrawLayout / FPPLayout

  • TutteLayout

  • DominanceLayout / VisibilityLayout (upward, for DAGs)

  • LinearLayout (arc diagram)

  • UML layouts (PlanarizationLayoutUML)

docs.title: Connectivity and structure
docs.text:
• is_connected, is_biconnected, is_triconnected

  • is_acyclic, is_acyclic_undirected

  • is_bipartite (with optional 2-coloring), is_tree, is_forest

  • connected_components, strong_components, biconnected_components

  • topological_numbering

  • make_connected, make_biconnected, make_acyclic

  • is_two_edge_connected, is_regular, is_arborescence

  • cut vertices (cut_vertices) and bridges (bridges)

  • triangulate, make_bimodal

  • Triconnectivity: separation pair (separation_pair) and SPQR-tree summary (spqr_tree_summary)

  • BC-trees / decomposition

docs.title: Planarity
docs.text:
• is_planar

  • planar_embed, planar_embed_planar_graph

  • Maximal planar subgraph (maximal_planar_subgraph)

  • Edge insertion / crossing minimization (as standalone API)

  • Upward planarity testing

  • Cluster planarity

docs.title: Shortest paths
docs.text:
• dijkstra (single-source, weighted)

  • A* search

  • Bellman-Ford (bellman_ford, negative weights)

  • Unweighted BFS distances (bfs_distances, single-source)

docs.title: Spanning trees, flow, cut
docs.text:
• min_spanning_tree (Prim), make_minimum_spanning_tree (Kruskal)

  • max_flow (Goldberg-Tarjan)

  • min_cut (Stoer-Wagner, global)

  • Max flow: Edmonds-Karp, planar s-t variants

  • Min-cost flow (min_cost_flow, Reinelt)

  • Min s-t cut variants

  • Nagamochi-Ibaraki min cut

docs.title: Matching and coloring
docs.text:
• maximal_matching

  • maximum_matching_bipartite

  • node_coloring (Recursive Largest First)

  • General maximum-weight matching (maximum_weight_matching, Blossom V)

  • Other coloring heuristics (Berger-Rompel, Johnson, Wigderson, ...)

docs.title: Excluded algorithm families
docs.text:
• Steiner trees (steiner_tree, Mehlhorn; OGDF has ~9 implementations)

  • Graph spanners (Baswana-Sen, Berman, Elkin-Neiman, ...)

  • PageRank

  • Voronoi diagrams / convex hull

  • Planar separators (Lipton-Tarjan, Har-Peled, ...)

  • Clustering (Clusterer, modified nibble)

  • Edge-independent spanning trees

  • Maximum density subgraph

  • Max adjacency ordering

docs.title: Generators
docs.text:
• complete_graph, complete_bipartite_graph

  • wheel_graph, cube_graph, grid_graph, petersen_graph

  • regular_tree, empty_graph

  • random_graph, random_tree, random_digraph

  • random_regular_graph, random_biconnected_graph

  • random_planar_connected_graph

  • circulant_graph, globe_graph, complete_kpartite_graph, regular_lattice_graph

  • Graph products: cartesian_product, tensor_product, strong_product, lexicographical_product

  • Random models: preferential_attachment_graph, random_chung_lu_graph, random_watts_strogatz_graph, random_waxman_graph

  • random_geometric_cube_graph, random_hierarchy, random_series_parallel_dag

  • random_triconnected_graph, random_planar_biconnected_graph, random_planar_triconnected_graph

  • Graph operations: graph_union, complement, suspension

docs.title: Interchange formats (read and write with attributes)
docs.text:
• GML (read_gml / write_gml)

  • GraphML (read_graphml / write_graphml)

  • DOT (read_dot / write_dot)

  • GEXF (read_gexf / write_gexf)

  • GDF (read_gdf / write_gdf)

  • TLP (read_tlp / write_tlp)

  • Generic read / write (format inferred from extension)

  • LEDA, Chaco (graph-only, no attributes)

  • DL, Rudy

  • Graph6 / Digraph6 / Sparse6

  • STP (Steiner), DMF (max-flow), Rome, benchmark formats

docs.title: Drawing output
docs.text:
• SVG (draw_svg / to_svg)

  • TikZ (draw_tikz / to_tikz)

docs.title: Excluded subsystems
docs.text:
Whole OGDF modules that are out of scope for the current curated subset:

  • Clustered graphs and cluster planarity / layout

  • UML diagram support

  • Hypergraphs

  • Upward planarity and upward drawing

  • Simultaneous embedding (SEFE) / SyncPlan

  • Augmentation (planarity / connectivity augmentation)

  • Rectangle / component packing

  • LP solver (COIN) interface

docs.title: Gallery
docs.text: 41 drawings generated by the demos. Regenerate with make demos.

docs.title: Layouts
docs.text: balloon circular dominance fmmm gem linear multilevel orthogonal pivot mds radial tree schnyder spring kk stress minimization sugiyama tree tutte visibility

docs.title: Algorithms
docs.text: bellman ford coloring components cut vertices matching min cost flow mst planar subgraph shortest paths steiner

docs.title: Generators
docs.text: bipartite k3 4 circulant 12 complete k6 cube 3 grid 5x5 hexagonal prism petersen preferential attachment random regular 3 regular tree watts strogatz wheel 10

docs.title: I/O
docs.text: sample

docs.title: From PyPI
docs.text:
pip install ogdf-py

Wheels are available for Linux (x86_64, aarch64), macOS (x86_64, arm64), and Windows (AMD64) on CPython 3.10-3.14. On platforms without a matching wheel, pip falls back to the source distribution, which builds OGDF from source at install time; this needs git, a C++17 compiler (MSVC works), CMake, and network access.

docs.title: From source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/shakfu/ogdf-py
cd ogdf-py
make bootstrap   # clone OGDF at the pinned tag and build it from source (once)
uv sync          # build the extension

make build and make sync run the bootstrap automatically if OGDF has not been built yet. See make help for all targets.

docs.title: Building graphs
docs.text:
import ogdf

g = ogdf.Graph()
a, b, c = g.new_node(), g.new_node(), g.new_node()
g.new_edge(a, b)
g.new_edge(b, c)

print(g.number_of_nodes(), g.number_of_edges())   # 3 2
for node in g.nodes():
    print(node.index, node.degree)

Generators fill a graph directly:

g = ogdf.Graph()
ogdf.complete_bipartite_graph(g, 3, 4)

docs.title: Layouts
docs.text:
Every layout writes coordinates into a GraphAttributes:

ga = ogdf.GraphAttributes(g)
layout = ogdf.FMMMLayout()
layout.set_unit_edge_length(20.0)
layout.call(ga)

docs.title: Styling and drawing
docs.text:
Enable the styling attributes, then set colors, shapes, and arrows:

ga = ogdf.GraphAttributes(g, ogdf.ALL_ATTRIBUTES)
ogdf.SugiyamaLayout().call(ga)

for v in g.nodes():
    ga.set_fill_color(v, ogdf.Color(70, 110, 200))
    ga.set_shape(v, ogdf.Shape.ELLIPSE)

svg = ogdf.to_svg(ga)          # or ogdf.draw_svg(ga, "graph.svg")
tikz = ogdf.to_tikz(ga)        # LaTeX / PGF output

docs.title: Algorithms
docs.text:
Algorithms operate on the Graph. Those that produce per-node or per-edge results follow OGDF's idiom: you pass in an array to receive the output, and the function returns the scalar result.

# Connected components -> count, plus a component id per node.
component = ogdf.NodeArrayInt(g)
n = ogdf.connected_components(g, component)

# Shortest paths from a source.
weight = ogdf.EdgeArrayDouble(g, 1.0)
dist = ogdf.NodeArrayDouble(g)
ogdf.dijkstra(g, weight, source, dist)

# Minimum spanning tree.
in_tree = ogdf.EdgeArrayBool(g)
total = ogdf.min_spanning_tree(g, weight, in_tree)

docs.title: File I/O
docs.text:
ogdf.write_gml(ga, "graph.gml")
ogdf.write_graphml(ga, "graph.graphml")

g2 = ogdf.Graph()
ga2 = ogdf.GraphAttributes(g2, ogdf.ALL_ATTRIBUTES)
ogdf.read_gml(ga2, g2, "graph.gml")

The generic write(ga, filename) chooses the format from the extension; it raises ValueError for formats that cannot store attributes.

docs.title: Demos
docs.text:
make demos

This writes example drawings and data files to build/demo-output/ and builds a self-contained index.html gallery.
